title: We need to rescue an incomplete project | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: We need to rescue an incomplete project | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halata...
seo.description: I help companies take over, stabilise, and restart incomplete software projects that are stuck between expectations, delivery, and technical reality.

page.title: We need to rescue an incomplete project | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: We need to rescue an incomplete project that has stalled
page.description: I help companies take over, stabilise, and restart incomplete software projects that are stuck between expectations, delivery, and technical reality.
page.primaryQuery: we need to rescue an incomplete project

hero.title: The project already cost time and money, but it is still not moving credibly
hero.subtitle: A common situation when scope expanded faster than delivery, trust in the current setup dropped, and the team no longer has a believable next milestone.

intro: An incomplete project is often harder than both greenfield work and takeover of a stable live product. It is not finished enough to rely on, but too far along to discard casually.
The stronger first move is to regain control of project reality and define the first trustworthy phase after the reset.

sections.title: Typical symptoms
body: If an incomplete project keeps moving without a reset of priorities and ownership, cost, frustration, and pressure for radical decisions usually keep rising without better clarity.
bullets: the project has no credible next step
buyer or team confidence in the current delivery model is low

sections.title: How I approach it
body: I start by re-grounding the reality: what exists, what is usable, what is risky, and what the first credible milestone should be. Only then does it make sense to decide what to keep, what to narrow, and what to drop.

sections.title: What a good outcome looks like
body: The goal is not a quick patch. The goal is to restore control, confidence, and a practical next step.
bullets: clearer project reality instead of wishful thinking
a believable next milestone after the reset
less chaos in scope and decision-making
stronger confidence in the next delivery phase

faq.question: Is it always better to stop and start again?
faq.answer: No. More often it makes sense to identify what can be kept, what needs stabilising, and what should be narrowed into a practical next phase.

faq.question: Can this work alongside an internal team?
faq.answer: Yes. Combining external takeover structure with internal domain knowledge is often the strongest path.

faq.question: Can you stay on as contract support after the reset?
faq.answer: Yes. Depending on the situation, I can continue through the delivery phase or work as senior support inside the team.

for: incomplete projects with no believable next milestone
buyers who already invested and need to regain control
situations requiring both takeover and a new delivery frame
notFor: projects with no access to code or brief
buyers looking only for validation of a pre-decided rewrite
recruiting-style requests with no project context

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the current state and the main risk is enough to start.
